result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
Result 0 is the seminal ICLR 2019 paper by Hendrycks & Dietterich introducing ImageNet-C and ImageNet-P. It directly addresses real-world deep learning failures under distribution shifts and common corruptions, making it highly relevant for the introduction and literature review sections to establish the baseline problem and motivate the workshop's focus on deployment pitfalls. Result 1 is an earlier preprint focusing on surface variations, but Result 0 is the definitive peer-reviewed publication that comprehensively covers corruptions and perturbations, so I will select it.
